title: Census Community Supporter Toolkit
content:
## Census Community Supporter Toolkit
Welcome to the 2026 Census of Population Community Supporter Toolkit
This toolkit provides helpful information and ready-to-use resources to promote the census and census job opportunities in your community. It includes materials that you and your organization can use to share the benefits and positive impact of the census.
We need your support to make the 2026 Census a success.

## What's in the toolkit?
You can download the following materials:
- Web banners and images
- Social media content

## Why support the census?
The Census of Population helps paint a statistical portrait of Canada's diverse population and the places where we live.
Results from the census help provide high-quality information on key socioeconomic trends and analysis that can support important decisions that affect organizations, businesses, municipalities, governments and Indigenous communities across Canada.
Your responses are vital for planning services that support employment, schools, public transportation, healthcare, and more.
Spread the word. Do you know of other organizations or associations who could use the Community Supporter Toolkit? Tell them about it.

## Encourage census completion
Help encourage census completion across Canada.
- Articles to encourage census completion — Read and share.
- Posters to encourage census completion — Printable posters to share in your community.
- Web images to encourage census completion — Add an image to your website or email signature.
- Social media posts to encourage census completion — Download and post on your social media to share with your followers.
Share with your network to help spread the word and encourage others to get involved.
- Articles to encourage census completion - It's not too late to complete your 2026 Census
- Articles to encourage census completion - It's not too late! Complete your 2026 Census today
- Articles to encourage census completion - There's still time to complete your census and we need your support!
- Articles to encourage census completion - Small Actions, Strong Communities
- Articles to encourage census completion - Three ways the census helps your community
Print and post these posters in high-traffic community spots to encourage census completion.
- Posters to encourage census completion - Poster — It's not too late to complete your 2026 Census!
- Posters to encourage census completion - Poster — It's not too late to complete your 2026 Census! (bilingual)
- Posters to encourage census completion - Poster — 2026 Census collection is underway across Canada
- Posters to encourage census completion - Postcard — The 2026 Census is underway
Support census collection efforts by adding these images to your email signature, website, or newsletter. When adding these images to your website or email signature, please link them to www.census.gc.ca .

#### Community supporter badge
- 2026 Census Proud Community Supporter (PNG, 7.82 KB)
- 2026 Census Proud Community Supporter (bilingual) (PNG, 7.27 KB)

#### Census web tile and banner
- Reminder - Your Census. Your Community. Our Future. (web tile) (PNG, 39.62 KB)
- Have you completed your 2026 Census (web tile) (PNG, 17.12 KB)
- Reminder - Your Census. Your Community. Our Future. (web banner) (PNG, 83.77 KB)
- Have you completed your 2026 Census (web banner) (PNG, 35.42 KB)
Use these ready-made posts to raise awareness and inform communities about the census.

#### Encourage census completion - Post 1
Haven't filled out your #2026Census questionnaire yet? It only takes a few minutes and makes a big difference. Census data paint a portrait of Canadians and their stories – from small communities to entire provinces and territories.
Complete yours today at census.gc.ca.
Download image (JPG, 308.27 KB) for Encourage census completion - post 1

#### Encourage census completion - Post 2
Census information is important and can help communities plan services such as child care, schools, and health care.
If you haven't completed your #2026Census, do so online today at census.gc.ca.
Download image (JPG, 132.46 KB) for Encourage census completion - post 2

#### Encourage census completion - Post 3
Have you completed your #2026Census? Your response matters! The census helps paint a statistical portrait of Canada's diverse population and the places where we live by collecting demographic, social and economic information from households across Canada.
You can help shape the future of your community with one simple action: complete your census online today at census.gc.ca.
Download image (JPG, 280.06 KB) for Encourage census completion - post 3

#### Encourage census completion - Post 4
The #2026Census is underway! The information you provide helps plan services in your community that support employment, transit, education, health care, and more. The information collected from the census will help provide Canadians with timely, quality data for the next 5 years!
Haven't filled out your questionnaire yet? There's still time, do it today! Learn more at census.gc.ca.
Download image (JPG, 309.51 KB) for Encourage census completion - post 4

#### Encourage census completion - Post 5
Have you completed your #2026Census? Census information is important for you and your community and is used to plan services that support employment, schools, public transportation and hospitals. Help plan your community's future.
Don't forget to complete your census! Visit: census.gc.ca.
Download image (JPG, 152.79 KB) for Encourage census completion - post 5

#### Encourage census completion - Post 6
Census employees will be visiting households across the country to follow up and offer assistance with completing #2026Census questionnaires.
Census employees will clearly identify themselves and have an identification card.
Learn more and complete your questionnaire at census.gc.ca.
Download image (JPG, 737.79 KB) for Encourage census completion - post 6

#### Encourage census completion - Post 7
There's still time to complete your #2026Census. For households who have not completed their census or submitted incomplete questionnaires, follow-up activities have begun across the country.
Thousands of census employees across the country are helping collect data that is vital in improving the lives of Canadians. These are your family, friends or neighbours, making a difference for your community.
The information you provide helps plan services in your community that support employment, transit, education, health care, and more. Learn more at census.gc.ca.
Download image (JPG, 667.47 KB) for Encourage census completion - post 7

## Indigenous communities
Encourage census completion and promote census jobs in Indigenous communities.
- Articles for Indigenous communities — Read and share.
- Posters for Indigenous communities — Printable posters to share in your community.
- Web images for Indigenous communities — Add an image to your website or email signature.
- Social media posts for Indigenous communities — Download and post on your social media to share with your followers.
Share with your network to help spread the word and encourage others to get involved.
- Articles for Indigenous communities - The 2026 Census is still underway in Indigenous communities!
- Articles for Indigenous communities - It's not too late to complete the 2026 Census!
- Articles for Indigenous communities - Census jobs available in Indigenous communities
- Articles for Indigenous communities - Apply now for a census job!
- Articles for Indigenous communities - Jobs available in Indigenous communities for the 2026 Census!
- Articles for Indigenous communities - Work that matters — Apply now!
- Articles for Indigenous communities - Local knowledge matters — Be part of the 2026 Census
Print and post these in high-traffic community spots to promote census collection activities and job opportunities.
- Posters for Indigenous communities - Poster — Census collection is underway in Indigenous communities
- Posters for Indigenous communities - Postcard — The 2026 Census is underway in Indigenous communities

- Posters for Indigenous communities - Postcard — Census jobs

#### Indigenous community supporter badge
Support census recruitment and collection efforts by adding these badges to your email signature, website, or newsletter. When adding these images to your website or email signature, please link them to www.census.gc.ca .
- 2026 Census Proud Community Supporter (with Indigenous symbols) (PNG, 8.25 KB)

#### Census jobs web tile and banner
Support recruitment efforts by adding these images to your email signature, website, or newsletter. When adding these images to your website or email signature, please link them to www.census.gc.ca/jobs .
- Statistics Canada is hiring! (web tile) (JPG, 57.17 KB)
- Statistics Canada is hiring in Indigenous communities (web banner) (PNG, 48.78 KB)

#### Encourage census completion
Use these ready-made posts to raise awareness and inform communities about the census.

#### Indigenous communities - Encourage census completion - Post 1
The #2026Census is underway in Indigenous communities. Did you know that the census questions are available in 15 Indigenous languages and dialects for reference purposes? Visit: census.gc.ca/many-languages for more information.
Don't forget to complete the census questionnaire: census.gc.ca.
Download image (JPG, 227.04 KB) for Indigenous communities - Encourage census completion - post 1

#### Indigenous communities - Encourage census completion - Post 2
It's not too late to complete the #2026Census!
For households in Indigenous communities that have not completed the census questionnaire or submitted an incomplete questionnaire, follow-up activities have begun. Census employees will clearly identify themselves and, when conducting in-person follow-ups, will carry official identification.
Complete the census today at census.gc.ca.
Download image (JPG, 267.42 KB) for Indigenous communities - Encourage census completion - post 2

#### Indigenous communities - Encourage census completion - Post 3
Have you completed the #2026Census questionnaire? With census data, Indigenous communities, organizations and businesses can use important information about population growth, languages spoken, education levels and much more to help in decision-making.
Don't forget to complete the census: census.gc.ca.
Download image (JPG, 297.75 KB) for Indigenous communities - Encourage census completion - post 3

#### Indigenous communities - Encourage census completion - Post 4
Collection for the #2026Census is underway in Indigenous communities across the country. Information collected through the census can help communities plan services such as child care, education, and health care. That's why it's so important that every community is counted.
There's still time to complete the census at census.gc.ca.
Download image (JPG, 275.54 KB) for Indigenous communities - Encourage census completion - post 4

#### Indigenous communities - Encourage census completion - Post 5
The #2026Census is underway in Indigenous communities. To ensure an accurate and representative picture of each community, it's important for each household to participate. Households can choose to complete their questionnaire online or with a Statistics Canada employee. Every census employee will clearly identify themselves and have an identification card.
Visit: census.gc.ca.
Download image (JPG, 146.15 KB) for Indigenous communities - Encourage census completion - post 5

#### Indigenous communities - Encourage census completion - Post 6
Census employees will be visiting households in Indigenous communities to follow up and offer assistance with completing #2026Census questionnaires. Census employees will clearly identify themselves and have an identification card. If contacting you by phone, the census employee will identify themselves clearly.
Learn more and complete the questionnaire at census.gc.ca.
Download image (JPG, 268.63 KB) for Indigenous communities - Encourage census completion - post 6
Use these ready-made posts to raise awareness and share job opportunities.

#### Indigenous communities - Promote census job - Post 1
Do you enjoy connecting with people and contributing to your community? Statistics Canada is hiring for the #2026Census. Apply now at census.gc.ca/jobs.
Download image (PNG, 644.66 KB) for Indigenous communities - Promote census job - post 1

#### Indigenous communities - Promote census job - Post 2
Statistics Canada is hiring in Indigenous communities across the country for the #2026Census. Your contribution to the census can help your community plan and shape its future—apply online at census.gc.ca/jobs.
Download image (PNG, 96.04 KB) for Indigenous communities - Promote census job - post 2

#### Indigenous communities - Post 3
Statistics Canada is hiring approximately 32,000 people across the country for the #2026Census. These temporary positions offer meaningful work that helps shape the future of Canadian communities. Apply now: census.gc.ca/jobs.
Download image (PNG, 1.05 MB) for Indigenous communities - Promote census job - post 3

#### Indigenous communities - Promote census job - Post 4
Statistics Canada is hiring enumerators and crew leaders for the #2026Census in Indigenous communities. Use your local knowledge to broaden your skills and gain professional experience. Apply now: census.gc.ca/jobs.
Download image (PNG, 952.52 KB) for Indigenous communities - Promote census job - post 4
Promote census jobs across Canada.
- Articles to promote census jobs — Read and share.
- Posters to promote census jobs — Printable posters to share in your community.
- Web images to promote census jobs — Add an image to your website or email signature.
- Social media posts to promote census jobs — Download and post on your social media to share with your followers.
Share with your network to help spread the word and encourage others to get involved.
- Articles to promote census jobs - Census jobs: A great way to develop skills that strengthen any career
- Articles to promote census jobs - Statistics Canada is hiring across Canada — Join the 2026 Census team!
- Articles to promote census jobs - Support your community — Apply for a census job!
Print and post these in high-traffic community spots to promote census job opportunities.
- Posters to promote census jobs - Poster — Census jobs
- Posters to promote census jobs - Postcard — Census jobs
Support recruitment efforts by adding these images to your email signature, website, or newsletter. When adding these images to your website or email signature, please link them to www.census.gc.ca/jobs .

#### Community supporter badge
- 2026 Census Proud Community Supporter (PNG, 7.82 KB)
- 2026 Census Proud Community Supporter (bilingual) (PNG, 7.27 KB)

#### Census jobs web tile and banner
- Statistics Canada is hiring! (web tile) (JPG, 50.24 KB)
- Statistics Canada is hiring across Canada (web banner) (PNG, 38.77 KB)
Use these ready-made posts to raise awareness and share job opportunities.

#### Promote census jobs - Post 1
Do you enjoy connecting with people? Want to contribute to your community? Statistics Canada is hiring for the #2026Census. These positions offer meaningful work that helps shape the future of communities across Canada. Apply now: census.gc.ca/jobs.
Download image (JPG, 634.48 KB) for Promote census jobs - post 1

#### Promote census jobs - Post 2
Statistics Canada is hiring approximately 32,000 people for the #2026Census, with jobs available across Canada. It's a great opportunity to gain experience, earn income, and support an important national initiative. Apply now: census.gc.ca/jobs.
Download image (JPG, 745.65 KB) for Promote census jobs - post 2

#### Promote census jobs - Post 3
Statistics Canada is hiring enumerators and crew leaders for Canada's largest survey, the #2026Census. Help collect the information that shapes programs and services that support employment, education, public transit, health care, and more. Apply now: census.gc.ca/jobs.
Download image (JPG, 819.60 KB) for Promote census jobs - post 3

#### Promote census jobs - Post 4
Job alert! Statistics Canada is hiring approximately 32,000 people across Canada for the #2026Census. These jobs offer competitive pay, valuable experience, flexible short-term work, and the chance to support an important effort that directly impacts your community. Apply now: census.gc.ca/jobs.
Download image (JPG, 861.72 KB) for Promote census jobs - post 4

#### Promote census jobs - Post 5
Statistics Canada is hiring enumerators and crew leaders for the #2026Census. Positions are available across Canada. Apply now: census.gc.ca/jobs.
Download image (JPG, 589.93 KB) for Promote census jobs - post 5

#### Promote census jobs - Post 6
Statistics Canada is hiring enumerators and crew leaders in rural communities across the country for the #2026Census. Flexible hours and competitive pay make it a great fit for many. Apply now: census.gc.ca/jobs.
Download image (JPG, 840.53 KB) for Promote census jobs - post 6

#### Promote census jobs - Post 7
Statistics Canada is hiring enumerators and crew leaders across the country. #2026Census workers help collect important information that supports health care, education, transportation, housing, and countless other programs people rely on. If you or someone you know is exploring new job opportunities, this is a chance to be part of a nation-wide effort that matters. Apply now and tell a friend: census.gc.ca/jobs.
Download image (JPG, 422.02 KB) for Promote census jobs - post 7
